history.text: Slovakia became independent from Austria-Hungary (First Czechoslovak Republic), marking a key step in modern statehood.
Treaty of Trianon set new terms for sovereignty or administration.
Second Czechoslovak Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
First Slovak Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Third Czechoslovak Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Fourth Czechoslovak Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Czechoslovak Socialist Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Slovak Socialist Republic (within Czechoslovak Socialist Republic, change of unitary Czechoslovak stat.

worthSeeing.title: High Tatras:
worthSeeing.text: The highest range in the Carpathians with Alpine peaks above 2,600 m.

worthSeeing.title: Spis Castle:
worthSeeing.text: One of the largest castle complexes in Central Europe, UNESCO listed.

worthSeeing.title: Bratislava Old Town:
worthSeeing.text: A compact and walkable Baroque capital perched above the Danube.
